parsing YAML\n50\t    #[error(\"Failed to parse config YAML in {path}: {}\", format_yaml_error(error))]\n51\t    ParseError {\n52\t        path: PathBuf,\n53\t        error: serde_yaml::Error,\n54\t    },\n55\t\n56\t    /// Config validation failed\n57\t    #[error(\"Config validation failed: {0}\")]\n58\t    ValidationError(String),\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\timpl From<String> for ConfigLoadError {\n62\t    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n63\t        ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(s)\n64\t    }\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\timpl ConfigLoadError {\n68\t    /// Get the line number where the error occurred (if available).\n69\t    pub fn line_number(&self) -> Option<usize> {\n70\t        match self {\n71\t            ConfigLoadError::ParseError { error, .. } => {\n72\t                error.location().map(|loc| loc.line())\n73\t            }\n74\t            _ => None,\n75\t        }\n76\t    }\n77\t\n78\t    /// Get the column number where the error occurred (if available).\n79\t    pub fn column_number(&self) -> Option<usize> {\n80\t        match self {\n81\t            ConfigLoadError::ParseError { error, .. } => {\n82\t                error.location().map(|loc| loc.column())\n83\t            }\n84\t            _ => None,\n85\t        }\n86\t    }\n87\t\n88\t    /// Get the config file path (if applicable).\n89\t    pub fn path(&self) -> Option<&PathBuf> {\n90\t        match self {\n91\t            ConfigLoadError::NotFound(path)\n92\t            | ConfigLoadError::ReadError { path, .. }\n93\t            | ConfigLoadError::ParseError { path, .. } => Some(path),\n94\t            _ => None,\n95\t        }\n96\t    }\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t/// Forge configuration structure.\n100\t///\n101\t/// This represents the subset of config.yaml that can be hot-reloaded.\n102\t/// Changes to these fields will take effect immediately.\n103\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\n104\tpub struct ForgeConfig {\n105\t    /// Dashboard configuration\n106\t    #[serde(default)]\n107\t    pub dashboard: DashboardConfig,\n108\t\n109\t    /// Theme configuration\n110\t    #[serde(default)]\n111\t    pub theme: ThemeConfig,\n112\t\n113\t    /// Cost tracking configuration\n114\t    #[serde(default)]\n115\t    pub cost_tracking: CostTrackingConfig,\n116\t\n117\t    /// Auto-recovery configuration\n118\t    #[serde(default)]\n119\t    pub auto_recovery: AutoRecoveryConfig,\n120\t\n121\t    /// Worker defaults configuration\n122\t    #[serde(default)]\n123\t    pub workers: WorkerConfig,\n124\t\n125\t    /// Notification configuration\n126\t    #[serde(default)]\n127\t    pub notifications: NotificationsConfig,\n128\t}\n129\t\n130\timpl ForgeConfig {\n131\t    /// Load configuration from the default path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\n132\t    ///\n133\t    /// Returns default configuration if the file doesn't exist or is invalid.\n134\t    /// Invalid configs are logged as warnings but don't prevent startup.\n135\t    pub fn load() -> Option<Self> {\n136\t        let path = config_path()?;\n137\t        Self::load_from(&path)\n138\t    }\n139\t\n140\t    /// Load configuration from the default path with detailed error reporting.\n141\t    ///\n142\t    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n143\t    pub fn load_with_error() -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n144\t        let path = config_path().ok_or(ConfigLoadError::NoHomePath)?;\n145\t        Self::load_from_with_error(&path)\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    /// Load configuration from a specific path with graceful fallback.\n149\t    ///\n150\t    /// This method attempts to load and parse the config file. If the file\n151\t    /// doesn't exist, is unreadable, or contains invalid YAML, it returns\n152\t    /// the default configuration rather than failing.\n153\t    ///\n154\t    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent the application from starting.\n155\t    pub fn load_from(path: &PathBuf) -> Option<Self> {\n156\t        if !path.exists() {\n157\t            tracing::debug!(\"Config file does not exist: {:?} - using defaults\", path);\n158\t            return None;\n159\t        }\n160\t\n161\t        // Try to read the file\n162\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n163\t            Ok(c) => c,\n164\t            Err(e) => {\n165\t                tracing::warn!(\n166\t                    path = ?path,\n167\t                    error = %e,\n168\t                    \"Failed to read config file - using defaults\"\n169\t                );\n170\t                return None;\n171\t            }\n172\t        };\n173\t\n174\t        // Try to parse with fallback to partial parsing\n175\t        Self::parse_with_fallback(&content)\n176\t    }\n177\t\n178\t    /// Load configuration from a specific path with detailed error reporting.\n179\t    ///\n180\t    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n181\t    pub fn load_from_with_error(path: &PathBuf) -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n182\t        if !path.exists() {\n183\t            return Err(ConfigLoadError::NotFound(path.clone()));\n184\t        }\n185\t\n186\t        // Try to read the file\n187\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n188\t            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ReadError {\n189\t                path: path.clone(),\n190\t                error: e,\n191\t            })?;\n192\t\n193\t        // Try to parse as full YAML\n194\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(&content) {\n195\t            Ok(config) => {\n196\t                // Validate and return errors if invalid\n197\t                config.validate()?;\n198\t                Ok(config)\n199\t            }\n200\t            Err(e) => Err(ConfigLoadError::ParseError {\n201\t                path: path.clone(),\n202\t                error: e,\n203\t            }),\n204\t        }\n205\t    }\n206\t\n207\t    /// Parse configuration from YAML string.\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// Returns None if parsing fails completely.\n210\t    pub fn parse(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n211\t        Self::parse_with_fallback(content)\n212\t    }\n213\t\n214\t    /// Parse configuration with fallback for partial/invalid configs.\n215\t    ///\n216\t    /// This method:\n217\t    /// 1. Tries to parse the full config\n218\t    /// 2. Falls back to partial parsing if sections are invalid\n219\t    /// 3. Returns default for completely invalid YAML\n220\t    fn parse_with_fallback(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n221\t        // First try to parse as full YAML\n222\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(content) {\n223\t            Ok(config) => {\n224\t                // Validate and warn about issues, but still return the config\n225\t                if let Err(e) = config.validate() {\n226\t                    tracing::warn!(\n227\t                        error = %e,\n228\t                        \"Config validation warning - some settings may be ignored\"\n229\t                    );\n230\t                }\n231\t                tracing::debug!(\"Successfully parsed forge config\");\n232\t                Some(config)\n233\t            }\n234\t            Err(e) => {\n235\t                // Format detailed error message with line/column information\n236\t                let error_msg = format_yaml_error(&e);\n237\t                tracing::warn!(\n238\t                    error = %error_msg,\n239\t                    \"Failed to parse config YAML - attempting partial parse\"\n240\t                );\n241\t\n242\t                // Try to parse individual sections as a fallback\n243\t                Self::parse_partial(content)\n244\t            }\n245\t        }\n246\t    }\n247\t\n248\t    /// Attempt to parse individual sections of a malformed config.\n249\t    ///\n250\t    /// This allows partial configs to work even if one section has errors.\n251\t    fn parse_partial(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n252\t        // Try to parse as generic YAML first\n253\t        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(content) {\n254\t            Ok(y) => y,\n255\t            Err(e) => {\n256\t                tracing::warn!(\n257\t                    error = %e,\n258\t                    \"Config is not valid YAML - using defaults\"\n259\t                );\n260\t                return None;\n261\t            }\n262\t        };\n263\t\n264\t        // Try to extract individual sections\n265\t        let dashboard = yaml\n266\t            .get(\"dashboard\")\n267\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n268\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n269\t\n270\t        let theme = yaml\n271\t            .get(\"theme\")\n272\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n273\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n274\t\n275\t        let cost_tracking = yaml\n276\t            .get(\"cost_tracking\")\n277\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n278\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n279\t\n280\t        let auto_recovery = yaml\n281\t            .get(\"auto_recovery\")\n282\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n283\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n284\t\n285\t        let workers = yaml\n286\t            .get(\"workers\")\n287\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n288\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n289\t\n290\t        let notifications = yaml\n291\t            .get(\"notifications\")\n292\t            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n293\t            .unwrap_or_default();\n294\t\n295\t        tracing::info!(\"Loaded partial config - some sections may use defaults\");\n296\t\n297\t        Some(Self {\n298\t            dashboard,\n299\t            theme,\n300\t            cost_tracking,\n301\t            auto_recovery,\n302\t            workers,\n303\t            notifications,\n304\t        })\n305\t    }\n306\t\n307\t    /// Validate the configuration.\n308\t    ///\n309\t    /// Returns Ok(()) if valid, Err with message if invalid.\n310\t    /// Warnings are logged but don't cause validation failure.\n311\t    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n312\t        let mut warnings = Vec::new();\n313\t\n314\t        // Validate refresh interval (must be >= 100ms)\n315\t        if self.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms < 100 {\n316\t            warnings.push(format!(\n317\t                \"refresh_interval_ms {} is too low, using minimum of 100ms\",\n318\t                self.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms\n319\t            ));\n320\t        }\n321\t\n322\t        // Validate max_fps (must be 1-120)\n323\t        if self.dashboard.max_fps == 0 || self.dashboard.max_fps > 120 {\n324\t            warnings.push(format!(\n325\t                \"max_fps {} is invalid, must be between 1 and 120\",\n326\t                self.dashboard.max_fps\n327\t            ));\n328\t        }\n329\t\n330\t        // Validate budget thresholds (must be 0-100)\n331\t        if self.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold > 100 {\n332\t            warnings.push(format!(\n333\t                \"budget_warning_threshold {} exceeds 100%\",\n334\t                self.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold\n335\t            ));\n336\t        }\n337\t\n338\t        if self.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold > 100 {\n339\t            warnings.push(format!(\n340\t                \"budget_critical_threshold {} exceeds 100%\",\n341\t                self.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold\n342\t            ));\n343\t        }\n344\t\n345\t        // Validate theme name if specified\n346\t        if let Some(ref theme_name) = self.theme.name {\n347\t            let valid_themes = [\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\"];\n348\t            if !valid_themes.contains(&theme_name.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n349\t                warnings.push(format!(\n350\t                    \"Invalid theme '{}', valid themes: {:?}\",\n351\t                    theme_name, valid_themes\n352\t                ));\n353\t            }\n354\t        }\n355\t\n356\t        // Validate max workers\n357\t        if self.workers.max_workers == 0 {\n358\t            warnings.push(\"max_workers must be at least 1\".to_string());\n359\t        }\n360\t\n361\t        // Validate default model\n362\t        let valid_models = [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"];\n363\t        if !valid_models.contains(&self.workers.default_model.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n364\t            warnings.push(format!(\n365\t                \"Invalid default_model '{}', valid models: {:?}\",\n366\t                self.workers.default_model, valid_models\n367\t            ));\n368\t        }\n369\t\n370\t        // Validate sonnet costs (must be non-negative)\n371\t        if self.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input < 0.0 {\n372\t            warnings.push(\"sonnet_cost_per_1k_input must be non-negative\".to_string());\n373\t        }\n374\t        if self.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output < 0.0 {\n375\t            warnings.push(\"sonnet_cost_per_1k_output must be non-negative\".to_string());\n376\t        }\n377\t\n378\t        if warnings.is_empty() {\n379\t            Ok(())\n380\t        } else {\n381\t            // Return the first warning as the error message\n382\t            Err(warnings.join(\"; \"))\n383\t        }\n384\t    }\n385\t\n386\t    /// Sanitize the configuration by fixing invalid values.\n387\t    ///\n388\t    /// Returns a new config with all invalid values replaced by defaults.\n389\t    pub fn sanitized(&self) -> Self {\n390\t        let mut config = self.clone();\n391\t\n392\t        // Sanitize refresh interval\n393\t        if config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms < 100 {\n394\t            tracing::warn!(\n395\t                original = config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms,\n396\t                \"Sanitizing refresh_interval_ms to minimum 100ms\"\n397\t            );\n398\t            config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms = 100;\n399\t        }\n400\t\n401\t        // Sanitize max_fps\n402\t        if config.dashboard.max_fps == 0 || config.dashboard.max_fps > 120 {\n403\t            tracing::warn!(\n404\t                original = config.dashboard.max_fps,\n405\t                \"Sanitizing max_fps to default 60\"\n406\t            );\n407\t            config.dashboard.max_fps = 60;\n408\t        }\n409\t\n410\t        // Sanitize budget thresholds\n411\t        if config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold > 100 {\n412\t            tracing::warn!(\n413\t                original = config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold,\n414\t                \"Sanitizing budget_warning_threshold to 100\"\n415\t            );\n416\t            config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold = 100;\n417\t        }\n418\t\n419\t        if config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold > 100 {\n420\t            tracing::warn!(\n421\t                original = config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold,\n422\t                \"Sanitizing budget_critical_threshold to 100\"\n423\t            );\n424\t            config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold = 100;\n425\t        }\n426\t\n427\t        // Sanitize theme name\n428\t        if let Some(ref theme_name) = config.theme.name {\n429\t            let valid_themes = [\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\"];\n430\t            if !valid_themes.contains(&theme_name.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n431\t                tracing::warn!(\n432\t                    original = theme_name,\n433\t                    \"Sanitizing invalid theme name to default\"\n434\t                );\n435\t                config.theme.name = None;\n436\t            }\n437\t        }\n438\t\n439\t        // Sanitize max workers\n440\t        if config.workers.max_workers == 0 {\n441\t            tracing::warn!(\n442\t                original = config.workers.max_workers,\n443\t                \"Sanitizing max_workers to minimum 1\"\n444\t            );\n445\t            config.workers.max_workers = 1;\n446\t        }\n447\t\n448\t        // Sanitize default model\n449\t        let valid_models = [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"];\n450\t        if !valid_models.contains(&config.workers.default_model.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n451\t            tracing::warn!(\n452\t                original = config.workers.default_model,\n453\t                \"Sanitizing invalid default_model to sonnet\"\n454\t            );\n455\t            config.workers.default_model = \"sonnet\".to_string();\n456\t        }\n457\t\n458\t        // Sanitize sonnet costs\n459\t        if config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input < 0.0 {\n460\t config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output = 0.015;\n464\t        }\n465\t\n466\t        config\n467\t    }\n468\t\n469\t    /// Save configuration to a file.\n470\t    ///\n471\t    /// Writes the configuration as YAML to the specified path.\n472\t    /// Creates parent directories if they don't exist.\n473\t    pub fn save_to(&self, path: &PathBuf) -> Result<(), ConfigLoadError> {\n474\t        // Create parent directory if needed\n475\t        if let Some(parent) = path.parent()\n476\t            && !parent.exists() {\n477\t                std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e|          ))?;\n487\t\n488\t        // Write to file\n489\t        std::fs::write(path, yaml)\n490\t            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n491\t                format!(\"Failed to write config file: {}\", e)\n492\t            ))?;\n493\t\n494\t        tracing::info!(\"Saved configuration to {:?}\", path);\n495\t        Ok(())\n496\t    }\n497\t\n498\t    /// Save configuration self.save_to(&path)\n502\t    }\n503\t}\n504\t\n505\t/// Dashboard configuration.\n506\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\n507\tpub struct DashboardConfig {\n508\t    /// Configuration management for FORGE.\n//!\n//! This crate handles loading, validating, and managing FORGE configuration\n//! from `~/.forge/config.yaml`.\n\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse thiserror::Error;\n\n/// Default config file path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\npub fn config_path() -> Option<PathBuf> {\n    dirs::home_dir().map(|h| h.join(\".forge/config.yaml\"))\n}\n\n/// Format a YAML parse error with line/column information if available.\nfn format_yaml_error(error: &serde_yaml::Error) -> String {\n    if let Some(location) = error.location() {\n        format!(\n            \"YAML parse error at line {}, column {}: {}\",\n            location.line(),\n            location.column(),\n            error\n        )\n    } else {\n        format!(\"YAML parse error: {}\", error)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Errors that can occur when loading configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum ConfigLoadError {\n    /// Could not determine home directory\n    #[error(\"Could not determine home directory\")]\n    NoHomePath,\n\n    /// Config file not found\n    #[error(\"Config file not found: {0}\")]\n    NotFound(PathBuf),\n\n    /// Error reading config file\n    #[error(\"Failed to read config file {path}: {error}\")]\n    ReadError {\n        path: PathBuf,\n        #[source]\n        error: io::Error,\n    },\n\n    /// Error parsing YAML\n    #[error(\"Failed to parse config YAML in {path}: {}\", format_yaml_error(error))]\n    ParseError {\n        path: PathBuf,\n        error: serde_yaml::Error,\n    },\n\n    /// Config validation failed\n    #[error(\"Config validation failed: {0}\")]\n    ValidationError(String),\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for ConfigLoadError {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ConfigLoadError {\n    /// Get the line number where the error occurred (if available).\n    pub fn line_number(&self) -> Option<usize> {\n        match self {\n            ConfigLoadError::ParseError { error, .. } => {\n                error.location().map(|loc| loc.line())\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the column number where the error occurred (if available).\n    pub fn column_number(&self) -> Option<usize> {\n        match self {\n            ConfigLoadError::ParseError { error, .. } => {\n                error.location().map(|loc| loc.column())\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the config file path (if applicable).\n    pub fn path(&self) -> Option<&PathBuf> {\n        match self {\n            ConfigLoadError::NotFound(path)\n            | ConfigLoadError::ReadError { path, .. }\n            | ConfigLoadError::ParseError { path, .. } => Some(path),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Forge configuration structure.\n///\n/// This represents the subset of config.yaml that can be hot-reloaded.\n/// Changes to these fields will take effect immediately.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct ForgeConfig {\n    /// Dashboard configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub dashboard: DashboardConfig,\n\n    /// Theme configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub theme: ThemeConfig,\n\n    /// Cost tracking configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub cost_tracking: CostTrackingConfig,\n\n    /// Auto-recovery configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub auto_recovery: AutoRecoveryConfig,\n\n    /// Worker defaults configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub workers: WorkerConfig,\n\n    /// Notification configuration\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub notifications: NotificationsConfig,\n}\n\nimpl ForgeConfig {\n    /// Load configuration from the default path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\n    ///\n    /// Returns default configuration if the file doesn't exist or is invalid.\n    /// Invalid configs are logged as warnings but don't prevent startup.\n    pub fn load() -> Option<Self> {\n        let path = config_path()?;\n        Self::load_from(&path)\n    }\n\n    /// Load configuration from the default path with detailed error reporting.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n    pub fn load_with_error() -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n        let path = config_path().ok_or(ConfigLoadError::NoHomePath)?;\n        Self::load_from_with_error(&path)\n    }\n\n    /// Load configuration from a specific path with graceful fallback.\n    ///\n    /// This method attempts to load and parse the config file. If the file\n    /// doesn't exist, is unreadable, or contains invalid YAML, it returns\n    /// the default configuration rather than failing.\n    ///\n    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent the application from starting.\n    pub fn load_from(path: &PathBuf) -> Option<Self> {\n        if !path.exists() {\n            tracing::debug!(\"Config file does not exist: {:?} - using defaults\", path);\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        // Try to read the file\n        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(c) => c,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    path = ?path,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"Failed to read config file - using defaults\"\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Try to parse with fallback to partial parsing\n        Self::parse_with_fallback(&content)\n    }\n\n    /// Load configuration from a specific path with detailed error reporting.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a Result with detailed error information for display to users.\n    pub fn load_from_with_error(path: &PathBuf) -> Result<Self, ConfigLoadError> {\n        if !path.exists() {\n            return Err(ConfigLoadError::NotFound(path.clone()));\n        }\n\n        // Try to read the file\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ReadError {\n                path: path.clone(),\n                error: e,\n            })?;\n\n        // Try to parse as full YAML\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(&content) {\n            Ok(config) => {\n                // Validate and return errors if invalid\n                config.validate()?;\n                Ok(config)\n            }\n            Err(e) => Err(ConfigLoadError::ParseError {\n                path: path.clone(),\n                error: e,\n            }),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Parse configuration from YAML string.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if parsing fails completely.\n    pub fn parse(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        Self::parse_with_fallback(content)\n    }\n\n    /// Parse configuration with fallback for partial/invalid configs.\n    ///\n    /// This method:\n    /// 1. Tries to parse the full config\n    /// 2. Falls back to partial parsing if sections are invalid\n    /// 3. Returns default for completely invalid YAML\n    fn parse_with_fallback(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        // First try to parse as full YAML\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<ForgeConfig>(content) {\n            Ok(config) => {\n                // Validate and warn about issues, but still return the config\n                if let Err(e) = config.validate() {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"Config validation warning - some settings may be ignored\"\n                    );\n                }\n                tracing::debug!(\"Successfully parsed forge config\");\n                Some(config)\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Format detailed error message with line/column information\n                let error_msg = format_yaml_error(&e);\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %error_msg,\n                    \"Failed to parse config YAML - attempting partial parse\"\n                );\n\n                // Try to parse individual sections as a fallback\n                Self::parse_partial(content)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Attempt to parse individual sections of a malformed config.\n    ///\n    /// This allows partial configs to work even if one section has errors.\n    fn parse_partial(content: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        // Try to parse as generic YAML first\n        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(content) {\n            Ok(y) => y,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"Config is not valid YAML - using defaults\"\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Try to extract individual sections\n        let dashboard = yaml\n            .get(\"dashboard\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let theme = yaml\n            .get(\"theme\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let cost_tracking = yaml\n            .get(\"cost_tracking\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let auto_recovery = yaml\n            .get(\"auto_recovery\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let workers = yaml\n            .get(\"workers\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let notifications = yaml\n            .get(\"notifications\")\n            .and_then(|v| serde_yaml::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        tracing::info!(\"Loaded partial config - some sections may use defaults\");\n\n        Some(Self {\n            dashboard,\n            theme,\n            cost_tracking,\n            auto_recovery,\n            workers,\n            notifications,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Validate the configuration.\n    ///\n    /// Returns Ok(()) if valid, Err with message if invalid.\n    /// Warnings are logged but don't cause validation failure.\n    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n        let mut warnings = Vec::new();\n\n        // Validate refresh interval (must be >= 100ms)\n        if self.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms < 100 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"refresh_interval_ms {} is too low, using minimum of 100ms\",\n                self.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate max_fps (must be 1-120)\n        if self.dashboard.max_fps == 0 || self.dashboard.max_fps > 120 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"max_fps {} is invalid, must be between 1 and 120\",\n                self.dashboard.max_fps\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate budget thresholds (must be 0-100)\n        if self.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold > 100 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"budget_warning_threshold {} exceeds 100%\",\n                self.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if self.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold > 100 {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"budget_critical_threshold {} exceeds 100%\",\n                self.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate theme name if specified\n        if let Some(ref theme_name) = self.theme.name {\n            let valid_themes = [\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\"];\n            if !valid_themes.contains(&theme_name.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n                warnings.push(format!(\n                    \"Invalid theme '{}', valid themes: {:?}\",\n                    theme_name, valid_themes\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Validate max workers\n        if self.workers.max_workers == 0 {\n            warnings.push(\"max_workers must be at least 1\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        // Validate default model\n        let valid_models = [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"];\n        if !valid_models.contains(&self.workers.default_model.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n            warnings.push(format!(\n                \"Invalid default_model '{}', valid models: {:?}\",\n                self.workers.default_model, valid_models\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Validate sonnet costs (must be non-negative)\n        if self.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input < 0.0 {\n            warnings.push(\"sonnet_cost_per_1k_input must be non-negative\".to_string());\n        }\n        if self.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output < 0.0 {\n            warnings.push(\"sonnet_cost_per_1k_output must be non-negative\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        if warnings.is_empty() {\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            // Return the first warning as the error message\n            Err(warnings.join(\"; \"))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Sanitize the configuration by fixing invalid values.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a new config with all invalid values replaced by defaults.\n    pub fn sanitized(&self) -> Self {\n        let mut config = self.clone();\n\n        // Sanitize refresh interval\n        if config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms < 100 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms,\n                \"Sanitizing refresh_interval_ms to minimum 100ms\"\n            );\n            config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms = 100;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize max_fps\n        if config.dashboard.max_fps == 0 || config.dashboard.max_fps > 120 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.dashboard.max_fps,\n                \"Sanitizing max_fps to default 60\"\n            );\n            config.dashboard.max_fps = 60;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize budget thresholds\n        if config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold > 100 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold,\n                \"Sanitizing budget_warning_threshold to 100\"\n            );\n            config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold = 100;\n        }\n\n        if config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold > 100 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold,\n                \"Sanitizing budget_critical_threshold to 100\"\n            );\n            config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold = 100;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize theme name\n        if let Some(ref theme_name) = config.theme.name {\n            let valid_themes = [\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\"];\n            if !valid_themes.contains(&theme_name.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    original = theme_name,\n                    \"Sanitizing invalid theme name to default\"\n                );\n                config.theme.name = None;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize max workers\n        if config.workers.max_workers == 0 {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.workers.max_workers,\n                \"Sanitizing max_workers to minimum 1\"\n            );\n            config.workers.max_workers = 1;\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize default model\n        let valid_models = [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"];\n        if !valid_models.contains(&config.workers.default_model.to_lowercase().as_str()) {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                original = config.workers.default_model,\n                \"Sanitizing invalid default_model to sonnet\"\n            );\n            config.workers.default_model = \"sonnet\".to_string();\n        }\n\n        // Sanitize sonnet costs\n        if config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input < 0.0 {\n            config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input = 0.003;\n        }\n        if config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output < 0.0 {\n            config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output = 0.015;\n        }\n\n        config\n    }\n\n    /// Save configuration to a file.\n    ///\n    /// Writes the configuration as YAML to the specified path.\n    /// Creates parent directories if they don't exist.\n    pub fn save_to(&self, path: &PathBuf) -> Result<(), ConfigLoadError> {\n        // Create parent directory if needed\n        if let Some(parent) = path.parent()\n            && !parent.exists() {\n                std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n                    format!(\"Failed to create config directory: {}\", e)\n                ))?;\n        }\n\n        // Serialize to YAML\n        let yaml = serde_yaml::to_string(self)\n            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n                format!(\"Failed to serialize config: {}\", e)\n            ))?;\n\n        // Write to file\n        std::fs::write(path, yaml)\n            .map_err(|e| ConfigLoadError::ValidationError(\n                format!(\"Failed to write config file: {}\", e)\n            ))?;\n\n        tracing::info!(\"Saved configuration to {:?}\", path);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Save configuration to the default path (~/.forge/config.yaml).\n    pub fn save(&self) -> Result<(), ConfigLoadError> {\n        let path = config_path().ok_or(ConfigLoadError::NoHomePath)?;\n        self.save_to(&path)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Dashboard configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct DashboardConfig {\n    /// Refresh interval in milliseconds.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_refresh_interval\")]\n    pub refresh_interval_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Maximum frames per second.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_fps\")]\n    pub max_fps: u64,\n\n    /// Default layout mode.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_layout\")]\n    pub default_layout: String,\n\n    /// Log retention period in days (0 = forever).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_log_retention_days\")]\n    pub log_retention_days: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for DashboardConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            refresh_interval_ms: default_refresh_interval(),\n            max_fps: default_max_fps(),\n            default_layout: default_layout(),\n            log_retention_days: default_log_retention_days(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn default_refresh_interval() -> u64 {\n    1000\n}\n\nfn default_max_fps() -> u64 {\n    60\n}\n\nfn default_layout() -> String {\n    \"overview\".to_string()\n}\n\nfn default_log_retention_days() -> u64 {\n    7\n}\n\n/// Theme configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct ThemeConfig {\n    /// Theme name (default, dark, light, cyberpunk).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub name: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Worker configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct WorkerConfig {\n    /// Maximum concurrent workers.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_workers\")]\n    pub max_workers: u64,\n\n    /// Default model for new workers.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_model\")]\n    pub default_model: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for WorkerConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            max_workers: default_max_workers(),\n            default_model: default_model(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn default_max_workers() -> u64 {\n    10\n}\n\nfn default_model() -> String {\n    \"sonnet\".to_string()\n}\n\n/// Cost tracking configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct CostTrackingConfig {\n    /// Whether cost tracking is enabled.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_cost_enabled\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n\n    /// Budget warning threshold (percentage).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_warning_threshold\")]\n    pub budget in USD.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub monthly_budget_usd: Option<f64>,\n\n    /// Cost per 1K input tokens for Sonnet (USD).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_sonnet_input_cost\")]\n    pub sonnet_cost_per_1k_input: f64,\n\n    /// Cost per 1K output tokens for Sonnet (USD).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_sonnet_output_cost\")]\n    pub sonnet_cost_per_1k_output: f64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for CostTrackingConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: default_cost_enabled(),\n            budget_warning_threshold: default_warning_threshold(),\n sonnet_cost_per_1k_output: default_sonnet_output_cost(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Auto-recovery configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct AutoRecoveryConfig {\n    /// Enable/disable auto-recovery.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n\n    /// How often to run recovery checks (in seconds).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_recovery_check_interval\")]\n    pub check_interval_secs: u64,\n\n    /// Policy for dead worker restart: \"disabled\", \"notify\", or \"auto\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_recovery_policy\")]\n    pub dead_worker_policy: String,\n\n    /// Maximum restart attempts for dead workers.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_restart_attempts\")]\n    pub max_restart_attempts: u8,\n\n    /// Policy for memory leak: \"disabled\", \"notify\", or \"auto\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_recovery_policy\")]\n    pub memory_leak_policy: String,\n\n    /// Memory threshold in MB before considering it a leak.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_memory_threshold\")]\n    pub memory_threshold_mb: u64,\n\n    /// Policy for stuck tasks: \"disabled\", \"notify\", or \"auto\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_recovery_policy\")]\n    pub stuck_task_policy: String,\n\n    /// Time in minutes before a task is considered stuck.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_stuck_timeout\")]\n    pub stuck_task_timeout_mins: i64,\n\n    /// Policy for stale assignees: \"disabled\", \"notify\", or \"auto\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_stale_assignee_policy\")]\n    pub stale_assignee_policy: String,\n\n    /// Time in minutes before an assignee is considered stale.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_stale_timeout\")]\n    pub stale_assignee_timeout_mins: i64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for AutoRecoveryConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: false,\n            check_interval_secs: default_recovery_check_interval(),\n            dead_worker_policy: default_recovery_policy(),\n            max_restart_attempts: default_max_restart_attempts(),\n            memory_leak_policy: #[serde(default = \"default_visual_flash\")]\n    pub visual_flash_on_critical: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for NotificationsConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_default_config() {\n        let config = ForgeConfig::default();\n        assert_eq!(config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms, 1000);\n        assert_eq!(config.dashboard.max_fps, 60);\n        assert!(config.cost_tracking.enabled);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_valid_config() {\n        let yaml = r#\"\ndashboard:\n  refresh_interval_ms: 500\n  max_fps: 30\n  default_layout: workers\n\ntheme:\n  name: cyberpunk\n\ncost_tracking:\n  enabled: true\n  budget_warning_threshold: 80\n  budget_critical_threshold: 95\n  monthly_budget_usd: 100.0\n\"#;\n        let config = ForgeConfig::parse(yaml).expect(\"Failed to parse config\");\n        assert_eq!(config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms, 500);\n        assert_eq!(config.dashboard.max_fps, 30);\n        assert_eq!(config.theme.name, Some(\"cyberpunk\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold, 80);\n        assert_eq!(config.cost_tracking.monthly_budget_usd, Some(100.0));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_partial_config() {\n        let yaml = r#\"\ndashboard:\n  refresh_interval_ms: 2000\n\"#;\n        let config = ForgeConfig::parse(yaml).expect(\"Failed to parse config\");\n        assert_eq!(config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms, 2000);\n        // Defaults should be used for missing fields\n        assert_eq!(config.dashboard.max_fps, 60);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_valid_config() {\n        let config = ForgeConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.validate().is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_invalid_refresh_interval() {\n        let mut config = ForgeConfig::default();\n        config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms = 50; // Too low\n        assert!(config.validate().is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_invalid_fps() {\n        let mut config = ForgeConfig::default();\n        config.dashboard.max_fps = 200; // Too high\n        assert!(config.validate().is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_invalid_theme() {\n        let mut config = ForgeConfig::default();\n        config.theme.name = Some(\"invalid_theme\".to_string());\n        assert!(config.validate().is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_valid_themes() {\n        for theme in &[\"default\", \"dark\", \"light\", \"cyberpunk\", \"DEFAULT\", \"CyberPunk\"] {\n            let mut config = ForgeConfig::default();\n            config.theme.name = Some(theme.to_string());\n            assert!(config.validate().is_ok(), \"Theme '{}' should be valid\", theme);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_default_notifications_config() {\n        let config = NotificationsConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.bell_on_critical);\n        assert!(!config.bell_on_warning);\n        assert_eq!(config.bell_interval_secs, 30);\n        assert!(config.visual_flash_on_critical);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_notifications_config() {\n        let yaml = r#\"\nnotifications:\n  bell_on_critical: false\n  bell_on_warning: true\n  bell_interval_secs: 60\n  visual_flash_on_critical: false\n\"#;\n        let config = test_notifications_config_defaults_when_missing() {\n        let yaml = r#\"\ndashboard:\n  refresh_interval_ms: 1000\n\"#;\n        let config = ForgeConfig::parse(yaml).expect(\"Failed to parse config\");\n        // Notifications should use defaults\n        assert!(config.notifications.bell_on_critical);\n        assert!(!config.notifications.bell_on_warning);\n Worker launcher implementation using tokio::process.\n//!\n//! This module provides the [`WorkerLauncher`] type for spawning worker processes\n//! in tmux sessions using configurable launcher scripts.\n\nuse crate::tmux;\nuse crate::types::{LaunchConfig, LauncherOutput, SpawnRequest, WorkerHandle};\nuse forge_core::types::WorkerStatus;\nuse forge_core::{ForgeError, Result};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::path::Path;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::process::Command;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tokio::time::timeout;\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument, warn};\n\n/// Worker launcher for spawning and managing worker processes.\n///\n/// The launcher uses external launcher scripts to spawn workers in tmux sessions.\n/// Launcher scripts must output JSON to stdout with worker information.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct WorkerLauncher {\n    /// Active worker handles keyed by worker ID\n    workers: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, WorkerHandle>>>,\n    /// Session name prefix for all workers\n    session_prefix: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for WorkerLauncher {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl WorkerLauncher {\n    /// Create a new worker launcher.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            workers: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            session_prefix: \"forge-\".into(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new worker launcher with a custom session prefix.\n    pub fn with_prefix(prefix: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            workers: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            session_prefix: prefix.into(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn a new worker using the provided configuration.\n    ///\n    /// This will:\n    /// 1. Validate the launcher script exists and is executable\n    /// 2. Execute the launcher script with appropriate environment\n    /// 3. Parse the JSON output to get PID and session info\n    /// 4. Create a WorkerHandle and track it\n    #[instrument(level = \"info\", skip(self), fields(worker_id = %request.worker_id, model = %request.config.model))]\n    pub async fn spawn(&self, request: SpawnRequest) -> Result<WorkerHandle> {\n        let config = &request.config;\n        let worker_id = &request.worker_id;\n\n        // Validate launcher exists\n        self.validate_launcher(&config.launcher_path).await?;\n\n        info!(\n            \"Spawning worker {} with model {} in {}\",\n            worker_id,\n            config.model,\n            config.workspace.display()\n        );\n\n        // Check if session already exists and kill it\n        let session_name = format!(\"{}{}\", self.session_prefix, config.session_name);\n        if tmux::session_exists(&session_name).await? {\n            warn!(\"Session {} already exists, killing it\", session_name);\n            tmux::kill_session(&session_name).await?;\n        }\n\n        // Execute the launcher script\n        let output = self\n            .execute_launcher(worker_id, config, &session_name)\n            .await?;\n\n        // Parse launcher output\n        let launcher_output = self.parse_launcher_output(worker_id, &output).await?;\n\n        // Validate the launcher succeeded\n        if !launcher_output.is_success() {\n            return Err(ForgeError::LauncherExecution {\n                model: config.model.clone(),\n                message: launcher_output\n                    .error\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|| \"Unknown launcher error\".into()),\n            });\n        }\n","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":531}}}

std::time::Instant,\n247\t}\n248\t\n249\timpl ContextProvider {\n250\t    /// Create a new context provider.\n251\t    pub fn new(source: impl ContextSource + 'static) -> Self {\n252\t        Self {\n253\t            source: Arc::new(source),\n254\t            cache: RwLock::new(None),\n255\t            cache_duration_secs: 5, // 5 second cache by default\n256\t        }\n257\t    }\n258\t\n259\t    /// Create with custom cache duration.\n260\t    pub fn with_cache_duration(mut self, secs: u64) -> Self {\n261\t        self.cache_duration_secs = secs;\n262\t        self\n263\t    }\n264\t\n265\t    /// Get the current context (from cache if fresh).\n266\t    pub async fn get_context(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n267\t        // Check cache\n268\t        {\n269\t            let cache = self.cache.read().await;\n270\t            if let Some(cached) = cache.as_ref()\n271\t                && cached.cached_at.elapsed().as_secs() < self.cache_duration_secs\n272\t            {\n273\t                return Ok(cached.context.clone());\n274\t            }\n275\t        }\n276\t\n277\t        // Gather fresh context\n278\t        let context = self.source.gather().await?;\n279\t\n280\t        // Update cache\n281\t        {\n282\t            let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n283\t            *cache = Some(CachedContext {\n284\t                context: context.clone(),\n285\t                cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n286\t            });\n287\t        }\n288\t\n289\t        Ok(context)\n290\t    }\n291\t\n292\t    /// Force refresh the context.\n293\t    pub async fn refresh(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n294\t        let context = self.source.gather().await?;\n295\t\n296\t        {\n297\t            let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n298\t            *cache = Some(CachedContext {\n299\t                context: context.clone(),\n300\t                cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n301\t            });\n302\t        }\n303\t\n304\t        Ok(context)\n305\t    }\n306\t\n307\t    /// Invalidate the cache.\n308\t    pub async fn invalidate(&self) {\n309\t        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n310\t        *cache = None;\n311\t    }\n312\t}\n313\t\n314\t/// Mock context source for testing.\n315\tpub struct MockContextSource {\n316\t    context: }\n323\t    }\n324\t\n325\t    /// Create with sample data.\n326\t    pub fn with_sample_data() -> Self {\n327\t        let context = DashboardContext {\n328\t            workers: vec![\n329\t   Self { context }\n411\t    }\n412\t}\n413\t\n414\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n415\timpl ContextSource for MockContextSource {\n416\t    async fn gather(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n417\t        Ok(self.context.clone())\n418\t    }\n419\t}\n420\t\n421\t/// Real context source that reads live data from the dashboard.\n422\t///\n423\t/// This implementation reads:\n424\t/// - Worker status from ~/.forge/status/*.json\n425\t/// - Task queue from the workspace's .beads/ directory via `br` CLI\n426\t/// - Cost data from ~/.forge/costs.db\n427\t/// - Recent events from the chat audit log\n428\tpub struct RealContextSource {\n429\t    /// Path to the forge status directory (~/.forge/status/)\n430\t    status_dir: PathBuf,\n431\t    /// Path to the cost database (~/.forge/costs.db)\n432\t    cost_db_path: PathBuf,\n433\t    /// Path to the subscriptions config (~/.forge/subscriptions.yaml)\n434\t    subscriptions_path: PathBuf,\n435\t    /// Path to the chat audit log (~/.forge/chat-audit.jsonl)\n436\t    audit_log_path: PathBuf,\n437\t    /// Workspace path for beads (current working directory or FORGE_WORKSPACE)\n438\t    workspace: PathBuf,\n439\t}\n440\t\n441\timpl RealContextSource {\n442\t    /// Create a new real context source with default paths.\n443\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n444\t        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/home/coder\".to_string());\n445\t        let forge_dir = PathBuf::from(&home).join(\".forge\");\n446\t\n447\t        // Get workspace from environment or current directory\n448\t        let workspace = std::env::var(\"FORGE_WORKSPACE\")\n449\t            .map(PathBuf::from)\n450\t            .unwrap_or_else(|_| {\n451\t                std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from(&home))\n452\t            });\n453\t\n454\t        Self {\n455\t            status_dir: forge_dir.join(\"status\"),\n456\t            cost_db_path: forge_dir.join(\"costs.db\"),\n457\t            subscriptions_path: forge_dir.join(\"subscriptions.yaml\"),\n458\t            audit_log_path: forge_dir.join(\"chat-audit.jsonl\"),\n459\t            workspace,\n460\t        }\n461\t    }\n462\t\n463\t    /// Create with custom paths (for testing).\n464\t    pub fn with_paths(\n465\t        status_dir: PathBuf,\n466\t        cost_db_path: PathBuf,\n467\t        subscriptions_path: PathBuf,\n468\t        audit_log_path: PathBuf,\n469\t        workspace: PathBuf,\n470\t    ) -> Self {\n471\t        Self {\n472\t            status_dir,\n473\t            cost_db_path,\n474\t            subscriptions_path,\n475\t            audit_log_path,\n476\t            workspace,\n477\t        }\n478\t    }\n479\t\n480\t    /// Read worker status files from the status directory.\n481\t    async fn read_workers(&self) -> Vec<WorkerInfo> {\n482\t        let mut workers = Vec::new();\n483\t\n484\t        // Read status directory\n485\t        let status_dir = self.status_dir.clone();\n486\t        let entries = match tokio::fs::read_dir(&status_dir).await {\n487\t            Ok(entries) => entries,\n488\t            Err(e) => {\n489\t                debug!(\"Failed to read status directory {:?}: {}\", status_dir, e);\n490\t                return workers;\n491\t            }\n492\t        };\n493\t\n494\t        let mut entries = entries;\n495\t        while let Ok(Some(entry)) = entries.next_entry().await {\n496\t            let path = entry.path();\n497\t            if path.extension().is_some_and(|ext| ext == \"json\") {\n498\t                match self.read_worker_status(&path).await {\n499\t                    Ok(worker) => workers.push(worker),\n500\t                    Err(e) => {\n501\t                        debug!(\"Failed to read worker status from {:?}: {}\", path, e);\n502\t                    }\n503\t                }\n504\t            }\n505\t        }\n506\t\n507\t        workers\n508\t    }\n509\t\n510\t    /// Read a single worker status file.\n511\t    async fn read_worker_status(&self, path: &PathBuf) -> std::io::Result<WorkerInfo> {\n512\t        let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(path).await?;\n513\t        let status: WorkerStatusFile = serde_json::from_str(&content)?;\n514\t\n515\t        // Parse timestamps\n516\t        let started_at = status.started_at.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n517\t            DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(s)\n518\t                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc))\n519\t                .ok()\n520\t        });\n521\t\n522\t        let last_activity = status.last_activity.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n523\t            DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(s)\n524\t                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc))\n525\t                .ok()\n526\t        });\n527\t\n528\t        // Calculate uptime\n529\t        let uptime_secs = if let Some(started) = started_at {\n530\t            (Utc::now() - started).num_seconds().max(0) as u64\n531\t        } else {\n532\t            0\n533\t        };\n534\t\n535\t        // Determine worker type from model name\n536\t        let worker_type = if status.model.contains(\"sonnet\") {\n537\t            \"sonnet\"\n538\t        } else if status.model.contains(\"opus\") {\n539\t            \"opus\"\n540\t        } else if status.model.contains(\"haiku\") {\n541\t            \"haiku\"\n542\t        } else if status.model.contains(\"glm\") {\n543\t            \"glm\"\n544\t        } else {\n545\t            \"unknown\"\n546\t        }\n547\t        .to_string();\n548\t\n549\t        // Check if healthy (active or idle status)\n550\t        let is_healthy = matches!(\n551\t            status.status.as_str(),\n552\t            \"active\" | \"idle\" | \"starting\"\n553\t        );\n554\t\n555\t        Ok(WorkerInfo {\n556\t            session_name: status.worker_id,\n557\t            worker_type,\n558\t            workspace: status.workspace.unwrap_or_default(),\n559\t            is_healthy,\n560\t            is_idle: status.status == \"idle\",\n561\t            current_task: status.current_task,\n562\t            uptime_secs,\n563\t            last_activity,\n564\t            beads_completed: status.tasks_completed.unwrap_or(0) as u32,\n565\t        })\n566\t    }\n567\t\n568\t    /// Read task queue from beads via br CLI.\n569\t    async fn read_tasks(&self) -> Vec<TaskInfo> {\n570\t        let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n571\t\n572\t        // Use br CLI to get ready tasks\n573\t        let output = tokio::process::Command::new(\"br\")\n574\t            .args([\"ready\", \"--format\", \"json\"])\n575\t            .current_dir(&self.workspace)\n576\t            .output()\n577\t            .await;\n578\t\n579\t        if let Ok(output) = output\n580\t            && output.status.success()\n581\t        {\n582\t            let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\n583\t            if !stdout.trim().is_empty()\n584\t                && stdout.trim() != \"[]\"\n585\t                && let Ok(beads) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<BeadJson>>(&stdout)\n586\t            {\n587\t                for bead in beads {\n588\t                    tasks.push(TaskInfo {\n589\t                        id: bead.id,\n590\t                        title: bead.title,\n591\t                        priority: format!(\"P{}\", bead.priority),\n592\t                        workspace: self.workspace.to_string_lossy().to_string(),\n593\t                        in_progress: false,\n594\t                        assigned_model: bead.assignee,\n595\t                        estimated_tokens: None,\n596\t                    });\n597\t                }\n598\t            }\n599\t        }\n600\t\n601\t        // Also get in-progress tasks\n602\t        let output = tokio::process::Command::new(\"br\")\n603\t            .args([\"list\", \"--status\", \"in_progress\", \"--format\", \"json\"])\n604\t            .current_dir(&self.workspace)\n605\t            .output()\n606\t            .await;\n607\t\n608\t        if let Ok(output) = output\n609\t            && output.status.success()\n610\t        {\n611\t            let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\n612\t            if !stdout.trim().is_empty()\n613\t                && stdout.trim() != \"[]\"\n614\t                && let Ok(beads) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<BeadJson>>(&stdout)\n615\t            {\n616\t                for bead in beads {\n617\t                    tasks.push(TaskInfo {\n618\t                        id: bead.id,\n619\t                        title: bead.title,\n620\t                        priority: format!(\"P{}\", bead.priority),\n621\t                        workspace: self.workspace.to_string_lossy().to_string(),\n622\t                        in_progress: true,\n623\t                        assigned_model: bead.assignee,\n624\t                        estimated_tokens: None,\n625\t                    });\n626\t                }\n627\t            }\n628\t        }\n629\t\n630\t        tasks\n631\t    }\n632\t\n633\t    /// Read cost data from the database.\n634\t    async fn read_costs(&self) -> (CostAnalytics, CostAnalytics) {\n635\t        // Use forge-cost to query costs\n636\t        let db = match forge_cost::CostDatabase::open(&self.cost_db_path) {\n637\t            Ok(db) => db,\n638\t            Err(e) => {\n639\t                debug!(\"Failed to open cost database: {}\", e);\n640\t                return (CostAnalytics::default(), CostAnalytics::default());\n641\t            }\n642\t        };\n643\t\n644\t        let query = forge_cost::CostQuery::new(&db);\n645\t\n646\t        // Get today's costs\n647\t        let today = query.get_today_costs().unwrap_or_else(|_| {\n648\t            forge_cost::DailyCost {\n649\t                date: chrono::Utc::now().date_naive(),\n650\t                total_cost_usd: 0.0,\n651\t                call_count: 0,\n652\t                total_tokens: 0,\n653\t                by_model: vec![],\n654\t            }\n655\t        });\n656\t        let costs_today = CostAnalytics {\n657\t            timeframe: \"today\".to_string(),\n658\t            total_cost_usd: today.total_cost_usd,\n659\t            by_model: today\n660\t                .by_model\n661\t                .iter()\n662\t                .map(|b| ModelCost {\n663\t                    model: b.model.clone(),\n664\t                    cost_usd: b.total_cost_usd,\n665\t                    percentage: if today.total_cost_usd > 0.0 {\n666\t                        (b.total_cost_usd / today.total_cost_usd) * 100.0\n667\t                    } else {\n668\t                        0.0\n669\t                    },\n670\t                })\n671\t                .collect(),\n672\t            by_priority: vec![], // Not tracked in forge-cost\n673\t        };\n674\t\n675\t        // Get projected costs\n676\t        let projected = query.get_projected_costs(None).unwrap_or(forge_cost::ProjectedCost {\n677\t            current_total: 0.0,\n678\t            daily_rate: 0.0,\n679\t            days_remaining: 0,\n680\t            projected_total: 0.0,\n681\t            confidence: 0.0,\n682\t        });\n683\t        let costs_projected = CostAnalytics {\n684\t            timeframe: \"month\".to_string(),\n685\t            total_cost_usd: projected.projected_total,\n686\t            by_model: vec![],\n687\t            by_priority: vec![],\n688\t        };\n689\t\n690\t        (costs_today, costs_projected)\n691\t    }\n692\t\n693\t    /// Read subscription data from config.\n694\t    async fn read_subscriptions(&self) -> Vec<SubscriptionInfo> {\n695\t        let content = match tokio::fs::read_to_string(&self.subscriptions_path).await {\n696\t            Ok(c) => c,\n697\t            Err(e) => {\n698\t                debug!(\"Failed to read subscriptions config: {}\", e);\n699\t                return vec![];\n700\t            }\n701\t        };\n702\t\n703\t        let config: SubscriptionsConfig = match serde_yaml::from_str(&content) {\n704\t            Ok(c) => c,\n705\t            Err(e) => {\n706\t                warn!(\"Failed to parse subscriptions config: {}\", e);\n707\t                return vec![];\n708\t            }\n709\t        };\n710\t\n711\t        config\n712\t            .subscriptions\n713\t            .into_iter()\n714\t            .filter(|s| s.active)\n715\t            .map(|s| {\n716\t                let usage_percentage = if let Some(limit) = s.monthly_tokens {\n717\t                    if limit > 0 {\n718\t                        (s.current_usage as f64 / limit as f64) * 100.0\n719\t                    } else {\n720\t                        0.0\n721\t                    }\n722\t                } else {\n723\t                    0.0\n724\t                };\n725\t\n726\t                let status = if usage_percentage >= 100.0 {\n727\t                    \"depleted\"\n728\t                } else if usage_percentage >= 90.0 {\n729\t                    \"max_out\"\n730\t                } else if usage_percentage < 50.0 {\n731\t                    \"accelerate\"\n732\t                } else {\n733\t                    \"on_pace\"\n734\t                };\n735\t\n736\t                let reset_time = if let Some(ref renewal) = s.renewal_date {\n737\t                    renewal.clone()\n738\t                } else {\n739\t                    \"monthly\".to_string()\n740\t                };\n741\t\n742\t                SubscriptionInfo {\n743\t                    name: s.provider,\n744\t                    quota_used: s.current_usage,\n745\t                    quota_limit: s.monthly_tokens,\n746\t                    reset_time,\n747\t                    status: status.to_string(),\n748\t                }\n749\t            })\n750\t            .collect()\n751\t    }\n752\t\n753\t    /// Read recent events from audit log.\n754\t    async fn read_recent_events(&self) -> Vec<EventInfo> {\n755\t        let content = match tokio::fs::read_to_string(&self.audit_log_path).await {\n756\t            Ok(c) => c,\n757\t            Err(e) => {\n758\t                debug!(\"Failed to read audit log: {}\", e);\n759\t                return vec![];\n760\t            }\n761\t        };\n762\t\n763\t        // Parse JSONL and get last 10 entries\n764\t        let mut events = Vec::new();\n765\t        for line in content.lines().rev().take(10) {\n766\t            if let Ok(entry) = serde_json::from_str::<AuditLogEntry>(line) {\n767\t                events.push(EventInfo {\n768\t                    timestamp: entry.timestamp,\n769\t                    event_type: if entry.success {\n770\t                        \"completions\".to_string()\n771\t                    } else {\n772\t                        \"error\".to_string()\n773\t                    },\n774\t                    description: entry.command,\n775\t                    worker: None,\n776\t                    bead_id: None,\n777\t                });\n778\t            }\n779\t        }\n780\t\n781\t        events\n782\t    }\n783\t}\n784\t\n785\timpl Default for RealContextSource {\n786\t    fn default() -> Self {\n787\t        Self::new()\n788\t    }\n789\t}\n790\t\n791\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n792\timpl ContextSource for RealContextSource {\n793\t    async fn gather(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n794\t        // Gather all data concurrently\n795\t        let (workers, tasks, (costs_today, costs_projected), subscriptions, recent_events) = tokio::join!(\n796\t            self.read_workers(),\n797\t            self.read_tasks(),\n798\t            self.read_costs(),\n799\t            self.read_subscriptions(),\n800\t            self.read_recent_events()\n801\t        );\n802\t\n803\t        Ok(DashboardContext {\n804\t            workers,\n805\t            tasks,\n806\t            costs_today,\n807\t            costs_projected,\n808\t            subscriptions,\n809\t            recent_events,\n810\t            timestamp: Utc::now(),\n811\t        })\n812\t    }\n813\t}\n814\t\n815\t/// Worker status file format (from ~/.forge/status/*.json).\n816\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\n817\tstruct WorkerStatusFile {\n818\t    worker_id: String,\n819\t    status: String,\n820\t    model: String,\n821\t    workspace: Option<String>,\n822\t    started_at: Option<String>,\n823\t    last_activity: Option<String>,\n824\t    current_task: Option<String>,\n825\t    tasks_completed: Option<i64>,\n826\t}\n827\t\n828\t/// Bead JSON format from br CLI.\n829\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\n830\tstruct BeadJson {\n831\t    id: String,\n832\t    title: String,\n833\t    priority: u8,\n834\t    #[serde(default)]\n835\t    assignee: Option<String>,\n836\t}\n837\t\n838\t/// Subscriptions config format.\n839\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\n840\tstruct SubscriptionsConfig {\n841\t    subscriptions: Vec<SubscriptionEntry>,\n842\t}\n843\t\n844\t/// Single subscription entry.\n845\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\n846\tstruct SubscriptionEntry {\n847\t    provider: String,\n848\t    #[serde(default)]\n849\t    active: bool,\n850\t    #[serde(default)]\n851\t    current_usage: i64,\n852\t    monthly_tokens: Option<i64>,\n853\t    renewal_date: Option<String>,\n854\t}\n855\t\n856\t/// Audit log entry format.\n857\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\n858\tstruct AuditLogEntry {\n859\t    timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n860\t    command: Context injection for dashboard state.\n\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, warn};\n\nuse crate::error::Result;\n\n/// Dashboard context for tool execution.\n///\n/// This structure provides the current state of the dashboard to tools,\n/// including worker status, task queue, costs, and subscriptions.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DashboardContext {\n    /// Current workers in the pool.\n    pub workers: Vec<WorkerInfo>,\n\n    /// Current task queue.\n    pub tasks: Vec<TaskInfo>,\n\n    /// Today's cost analytics.\n    pub costs_today: CostAnalytics,\n\n    /// Projected costs for the month.\n    pub costs_projected: CostAnalytics,\n\n    /// Subscription usage.\n    pub subscriptions: Vec<SubscriptionInfo>,\n\n    /// Recent events.\n    pub recent_events: Vec<EventInfo>,\n\n    /// Context timestamp.\n    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl DashboardContext {\n    /// Create a new empty context.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            timestamp: Utc::now(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Format context as a summary for the AI prompt.\n    pub fn to_summary(&self) -> String {\n        let mut summary = String::new();\n\n        // Workers summary\n        let healthy = self.workers.iter().filter(|w| w.is_healthy).count();\n        let idle = self.workers.iter().filter(|w| w.is_idle).count();\n        summary.push_str(&format!(\n            \"Workers: {} total ({} healthy, {} idle)\\n\",\n            self.workers.len(),\n            healthy,\n            idle\n        ));\n\n        // Tasks summary\n        let in_progress = self.tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.in_progress).count();\n        summary.push_str(&format!(\n            \"Tasks: {} ready, {} in progress\\n\",\n            self.tasks.len(),\n            in_progress\n        ));\n\n        // Costs summary\n        summary.push_str(&format!(\n            \"Costs today: ${:.2}\\n\",\n            self.costs_today.total_cost_usd\n        ));\n\n        // Subscriptions summary\n        for sub in &self.subscriptions {\n            if let Some(limit) = sub.quota_limit {\n                summary.push_str(&format!(\n                    \"{}: {}/{} ({:.0}%)\\n\",\n                    sub.name,\n                    sub.quota_used,\n                    limit,\n                    (sub.quota_used as f64 / limit as f64) * 100.0\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n\n        summary\n    }\n}\n\n/// Worker information for context.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WorkerInfo {\n    /// Session name.\n    pub session_name: String,\n\n    /// Worker type (e.g., \"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"glm\").\n    pub worker_type: String,\n\n    /// Workspace path.\n    pub workspace: String,\n\n    /// Whether the worker is healthy.\n    pub is_healthy: bool,\n\n    /// Whether the worker is idle.\n    pub is_idle: bool,\n\n    /// Current task (if any).\n    pub current_task: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Uptime in seconds.\n    pub uptime_secs: u64,\n\n    /// Last activity timestamp.\n    pub last_activity: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n\n    /// Beads completed.\n    pub beads_completed: u32,\n}\n\n/// Task information for context.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TaskInfo {\n    /// Task/bead ID.\n    pub id: String,\n\n    /// Task title.\n    pub title: String,\n\n    /// Priority (P0-P4).\n    pub priority: String,\n\n    /// Workspace path.\n    pub workspace: String,\n\n    /// Whether the task is in progress.\n    pub in_progress: bool,\n\n    /// Assigned model (if any).\n    pub assigned_model: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Estimated tokens.\n    pub estimated_tokens: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Cost analytics for context.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CostAnalytics {\n    /// Time period.\n    pub timeframe: String,\n\n    /// Total cost in USD.\n    pub total_cost_usd: f64,\n\n    /// Cost by model.\n    pub by_model: Vec<ModelCost>,\n\n    /// Cost by priority.\n    pub by_priority: Vec<PriorityCost>,\n}\n\n/// Cost breakdown by model.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ModelCost {\n    /// Model name.\n    pub model: String,\n\n    /// Cost in USD.\n    pub cost_usd: f64,\n\n    /// Percentage of total.\n    pub percentage: f64,\n}\n\n/// Cost breakdown by priority.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PriorityCost {\n    /// Priority level.\n    pub priority: String,\n\n    /// Cost in USD.\n    pub cost_usd: f64,\n}\n\n/// Subscription information for context.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SubscriptionInfo {\n    /// Subscription name.\n    pub name: String,\n\n    /// Quota used.\n    pub quota_used: i64,\n\n    /// Quota limit.\n    pub quota_limit: Option<i64>,\n\n    /// Time until reset.\n    pub reset_time: String,\n\n    /// Status (on_pace, accelerate, max_out, depleted).\n    pub status: String,\n}\n\n/// Event information for activity log.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct EventInfo {\n    /// Event timestamp.\n    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n\n    /// Event type.\n    pub event_type: String,\n\n    /// Event description.\n    pub description: String,\n\n    /// Related worker (if any).\n    pub worker: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Related bead (if any).\n    pub bead_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Provider for dashboard context.\n///\n/// This trait allows different implementations for gathering context,\n/// such as live data from the system or mock data for testing.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\npub trait ContextSource: Send + Sync {\n    /// Gather the current dashboard context.\n    async fn gather(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext>;\n}\n\n/// Context provider that caches context for a short duration.\npub struct ContextProvider {\n    source: Arc<dyn ContextSource>,\n    cache: RwLock<Option<CachedContext>>,\n    cache_duration_secs: u64,\n}\n\nstruct CachedContext {\n    context: DashboardContext,\n    cached_at: std::time::Instant,\n}\n\nimpl ContextProvider {\n    /// Create a new context provider.\n    pub fn new(source: impl ContextSource + 'static) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            source: Arc::new(source),\n            cache: RwLock::new(None),\n            cache_duration_secs: 5, // 5 second cache by default\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create with custom cache duration.\n    pub fn with_cache_duration(mut self, secs: u64) -> Self {\n        self.cache_duration_secs = secs;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current context (from cache if fresh).\n    pub async fn get_context(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n        // Check cache\n        {\n            let cache = self.cache.read().await;\n            if let Some(cached) = cache.as_ref()\n                && cached.cached_at.elapsed().as_secs() < self.cache_duration_secs\n            {\n                return Ok(cached.context.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Gather fresh context\n        let context = self.source.gather().await?;\n\n        // Update cache\n        {\n            let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n            *cache = Some(CachedContext {\n                context: context.clone(),\n                cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(context)\n    }\n\n    /// Force refresh the context.\n    pub async fn refresh(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n        let context = self.source.gather().await?;\n\n        {\n            let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n            *cache = Some(CachedContext {\n                context: context.clone(),\n                cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(context)\n    }\n\n    /// Invalidate the cache.\n    pub async fn invalidate(&self) {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        *cache = None;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Mock context source for testing.\npub struct MockContextSource {\n    context: DashboardContext,\n}\n\nimpl MockContextSource {\n    /// Create a new mock context source.\n    pub fn new(context: DashboardContext) -> Self {\n        Self {       worker_type: \"glm\".to_string(),\n                    workspace: \"/home/coder/forge\".to_string(),\n                    is_healthy: true,\n                    is_idle: true,\n                    current_task: None,\n                    uptime_secs: 1800,\n                    last_activity: Some(Utc::now()),\n                    beads_completed: 3,\n                },\n            ],\n       }],\n            costs_today: CostAnalytics {\n                timeframe: \"today\".to_string(),\n                total_cost_usd: 12.43,\n                by_model: vec![\n
